name: Aleut

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Aleut (aleu1260).

notes: Skeleton. Eskimo-Aleut (Aleut/Unangam) language of the Aleutian Islands written in the Bergsland practical orthography. Long vowels are doubled (aa ii uu); ng=[ŋ], d=[ð], g=[ɣ] with circumflexed x̂=[χ] and ĝ=[ʁ] for the uvular fricatives, and l̂=[ɬ] are mapped. The h-prefixed voiceless sonorants (hm hn hng hl hw hy) are mapped to voiceless counterparts. Predictable stress is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Aleut (aleu1260)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
